evals.name: rice-scoring-backlog
evals.prompt:
Prioritise these 5 backlog items using RICE:
1. Bulk export (requested by 3 enterprise customers)
2. SSO integration (blocking 2 enterprise deals worth $120K ARR)
3. Dashboard performance (page load >5s, affecting all users)
4. Mobile app notifications (nice to have, low effort)
5. API rate limiting (technical debt, no customer requests)
evals.expected_output: RICE scoring table with assumptions shown, three mandatory challenges completed, and quarterly priority decision output.
expectations: RICE table with Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort, Score, and Rank columns — the table format enables stakeholders to challenge individual scores rather than debating abstract priorities
All scoring assumptions are visible (not just numbers) — a RICE score from hidden assumptions is worse than no score because it creates false precision
Strategic Override Test completed — SSO may be an override candidate given the $120K ARR deal dependency, which is a legitimate strategic override that should be documented
Data Gap Test identifies low-confidence items and recommends discovery spikes — low-confidence, high-effort items committed to sprints are the #1 source of wasted engineering capacity
What Would We Regret test completed — this intuition check catches errors that framework scores reliably miss, like underscoring a burning platform issue
EXPLICITLY NOT BUILDING list is present — the not-building list is as important as the build list because it prevents zombie items from reappearing each quarter
Discovery spike recommended for any low-confidence, high-effort item — discovery before commitment is cheaper than pivoting mid-sprint

evals.name: single-feature-evaluation
evals.prompt: A key customer is asking us to build a Slack integration. They say they'll churn without it. Should we build it? It would take about 3 sprints.
evals.expected_output: A single feature evaluation with recommendation (BUILD / DEFER / DECLINE / DISCOVERY SPIKE), including opportunity cost analysis.
expectations: Uses the single feature evaluation format, not the full RICE framework — a yes/no decision on one feature requires different analysis than ranking a backlog
Includes opportunity cost — 'what does NOT get built if we build this?' is the question most feature requests fail to consider
Frequency of request is assessed (one customer vs pattern) — building for one customer's threat is different from building for a market pattern
Confidence in value is rated with basis — how sure are we that building this actually prevents the churn?
Recommendation is one of BUILD / DEFER / DECLINE / DISCOVERY SPIKE — not a wishy-washy 'it depends'

evals.name: value-vs-effort-quick-sort
evals.prompt: I don't need a complex framework. Just help me do a quick value vs effort 2x2 for these items: notification preferences, bulk user import, SSO, dark mode, API webhooks, custom dashboard layouts.
evals.expected_output: A Value vs Effort 2x2 matrix placing items into quadrants (Quick Wins, Strategic Bets, Fill-ins, Avoid).
expectations: Uses Value vs Effort matrix as requested, not RICE — the user explicitly asked for a simpler framework and should get one

Items are placed in four quadrants (high-value/low-effort, high-value/high-effort, low-value/low-effort, low-value/high-effort) — the quadrant labels help stakeholders immediately see what category each item falls into
Assumptions behind value and effort estimates are visible — even a 2x2 can mislead if the placement rationale is hidden
The three mandatory challenges are still run after the framework — quick frameworks are more prone to blind spots, not less

evals.name: negative-sprint-planning
evals.prompt: Help me plan our next sprint. I need to decide which stories to pull in based on team velocity of 42 points.
evals.expected_output: The skill should redirect to /sprint-planning — this is sprint capacity planning, not strategic prioritisation.
expectations: The skill does NOT perform sprint planning — sprint planning involves capacity allocation and story point math, not strategic prioritisation
Redirects to /sprint-planning for sprint-level work allocation

evals.name: negative-metrics-review
evals.prompt: Review our product metrics for Q2. What's our MRR, churn rate, and NPS trend?
evals.expected_output: The skill should redirect to /metrics-review — this is a metrics review request, not a prioritisation exercise.
expectations: The skill does NOT attempt to review metrics — metrics review is a separate workflow from prioritisation
Redirects to /metrics-review for product analytics review
